schema_notes.ref_classification_equivalent_group: Composite PK (scheme_id, group_id) — no id column. Maps a classification scheme to a sustainability action group. Columns: scheme_id, group_id, relation_type, requirement_type, match_scope, note. No name/i18n columns — i18n N/A.
schema_notes.ref_classification_equivalent_action: Composite PK (scheme_id, action_id) — no id column. Maps a classification scheme to a sustainability action. Columns: scheme_id, action_id, relation_type, requirement_type, match_scope, note. No name/i18n columns — i18n N/A.
schema_notes.ref_sustainability_action: Label text stored in 'label' column (NOT 'name'). i18n in label_i18n / description_i18n. category_id is a denormalized FK (redundant with group_id→group.category_id).

findings.title: Accessibility vocabulary (21 MA_* actions + 7 SA_* groups) seeded in source but absent from live DB
findings.evidence: seeds_data.sql embeds seeds_accessibility_v5.sql from approximately line 2406 onward. The accessibility action groups (SA_ACCESLIBRE, SA_ACCESSIBLE_PARKING, SA_ACCESSIBLE_PATHWAYS, SA_ACCESSIBLE_WEBSITE, SA_ACCESS_INFO, SA_ACCESS_REGISTER, SA_STAFF_TRAINING_ACCESS) and 21 MA_* micro-actions (MA_ACCESLIBRE_CREATED, MA_ACCESLIBRE_PHOTOS, MA_ACCESLIBRE_UPDATED, MA_ACCESSIBILITY_DECLARATION, MA_ACCESS_DEROGATIONS, MA_ACCESS_FACTSHEET, MA_ACCESS_PHOTOS, MA_ACCESS_REFERENT, MA_ACCESS_ROLEPLAY, MA_CORRECTION_PLAN, MA_HANDICAP_TRAINING, MA_PMR_SIGNAGE, MA_PMR_SLOPE_CHECK, MA_PMR_SPACES, MA_REGISTER_AVAILABLE, MA_REGISTER_UPDATED, MA_RGAA_AUDIT, MA_STAFF_KNOWS_REGISTER, MA_STEP_REMOVAL, MA_TACTILE_GUIDANCE, MA_WIDTH_120CM) are present in seeds_data.sql but absent from live DB. Live has 76 groups vs 83 in source; 239 actions vs 260 in source. 0 live-only codes — live is a clean subset of source.
findings.recommendation: Apply the accessibility seed block: these rows are needed before the PMV-001 label eligibility rail can evaluate Tourisme & Handicap coverage. Track as a pre-condition dependency in the deferred items list (seeds_accessibility_v5.sql application). Also verify: the 7 missing SA_* groups would need corresponding entries in ref_classification_equivalent_group if LBL_TOURISME_HANDICAP is to be scored by v_object_classification_coverage (currently that scheme has 0 equiv_group and 0 equiv_action links).

findings.title: LBL_TOURISME_HANDICAP has 0 rows in ref_classification_equivalent_group and ref_classification_equivalent_action — excluded from v_object_classification_coverage
findings.evidence: Query confirmed: LBL_TOURISME_HANDICAP (the only accessibility label in ref_classification_scheme) has has_equiv_group=false, has_equiv_action=false. The coverage view (v_object_classification_coverage) and search-expansion view (v_object_classification_or_equivalent_scheme) will never score any object against this label via sustainability actions. This is consistent with the 7 missing SA_* groups (C8-D4-01) — the links cannot be seeded until the groups exist. BUT: 4 objects already have LBL_TOURISME_HANDICAP classifications (4 rows in object_classification via scheme LBL_TOURISME_HANDICAP), so the label is in active production use.
findings.recommendation: Once the accessibility SA_* groups and MA_* actions are applied (C8-D4-01), seed the corresponding ref_classification_equivalent_group and ref_classification_equivalent_action rows for LBL_TOURISME_HANDICAP. Without this, object_sustainability_action data tagged to accessibility MA_ codes will never surface in coverage queries for that label. Document as a pre-condition dependency in lot1_mapping_decisions.md.

findings.title: 228/239 sustainability actions (95.4%) have zero usage in object_sustainability_action — large forward-looking vocabulary with thin data coverage
findings.evidence: object_sustainability_action has 51 rows using only 11 distinct action codes. 228 actions are unused. The 11 used codes are: MA_COMMON_BINS(14 objects), MA_MONTHLY_ENERGY_READING(9), MA_WATER_READING(7), MA_ONTRIP_BRIEFING(5), MA_ORGANIC_PRODUCTS(4), MA_ONSITE_COMPOST(3), MA_LOCAL_SUPPLIER_LIST(2), MA_EV_CHARGING(2), MA_WATERLESS_URINALS(2), MA_BIODIVERSITY_AWARENESS(2), MA_FURNITURE_REUSE(1). The action vocabulary (239 MA_* codes) is intentionally comprehensive for label-coverage mapping; usage is expected to grow during the CSV import phase (staging_d_durable — still deferred).
findings.recommendation: Classify as forward-looking (not dead code). The unused codes are needed for the coverage engine. No cleanup warranted. Confirm once staging_d_durable import is complete that at least the most common label-mapped actions get populated.

findings.title: 7 classification schemes have 0 object_classification rows — LBL_ATR, LBL_CLEF_VERTE, LBL_DESTINATION_EXCELLENCE, LBL_FLOCON_VERT, LBL_GREEN_DESTINATIONS, LBL_LABEL_BAS_CARBONE, LBL_PAVILLON_BLEU are forward-looking; camp_stars/retail_category/type_act/type_hot are typed but unused
findings.evidence: Query confirmed: 11 schemes have 0 object_classification rows. The 7 sustainability LBL_* schemes (excluding LBL_ECO_LABEL_UE with 2 rows, LBL_TOURISME_HANDICAP with 4, LBL_QUALITE_TOURISME with 1) have no data yet — these are pre-seeded for future label assignments. camp_stars (0 camping objects in current pilot), type_act/type_hot (no ACT/HOT objects using this specific classification sub-typing yet), retail_category (COM type newly added §57).
findings.recommendation: All forward-looking. No cleanup warranted for sustainability LBL_* schemes (needed for coverage view). type_act/type_hot usage will grow when ACT/HOT object data is enriched. camp_stars = no camping objects in pilot scope.

findings.title: Entire sustainability vocabulary is FR-only — no en/es/zh/el translations in label_i18n/name_i18n
findings.evidence: ref_sustainability_action: 239/239 have label_i18n={"fr":"..."} but 0 have 'en', 'es', 'zh', or 'el'. ref_sustainability_action_category: 9/9 have name_i18n={"fr":"..."} only. ref_sustainability_action_group: 76/76 have name_i18n={"fr":"..."} only. Seeds confirmed: no en/es/zh/el rows added by seeds_data.sql or seeds_sustainability_v5.sql for these tables. Comment at seeds_data.sql line 1825: 'V5 micro-actions (MA_*) have label_i18n and description_i18n set inline in SECTION A' — but only 'fr' is populated inline.
findings.recommendation: Document as a deferred i18n gap consistent with the post-MVP FR-fallback policy (decision log: 'FR-fallback at launch — decided 2026-06-02'). The seeds_data.sql line 1822/1825 comments correctly note the V5 inline i18n replaces the old translation block, but the inline population only covers 'fr'. Priority: add en/es at minimum for the 9 categories + 76 groups (they surface in the editor/explorer UI); MA_* labels are lower priority (internal tool use). Track under the existing 'Establishment-content translation' deferred item.

findings.title: ref_classification_scheme: 14/24 schemes have no name_i18n at all; ref_classification_value: 32/81 values have no i18n; zh and el missing for all 81 values
findings.evidence: ref_classification_scheme: 10/24 have name_i18n (all 9 sustainability LBL_* + 1 accessibility = {"fr":"..."} only). 14 operational schemes (hot_stars, camp_stars, meuble_stars, gites_epics, clevacances_keys, qualite_tourisme_reunion, maitre_restaurateur, esprit_parc, cte, bienvenue_ferme, accueil_paysan, retail_category, type_act, type_hot) have name_i18n=NULL. ref_classification_value: 35/81 have 'en', 35/81 have 'es' (the numeric star/key/epi values via the seeds_data.sql translations block line 1756–1801). 0/81 have zh or el. 46 values (cte/esprit_parc/maitre_restaurateur/qualite_tourisme_reunion singletons, all LBL_* granted values, accueil_paysan/bienvenue_ferme categories, retail_category values, type_act values) have no en/es.
findings.recommendation: en+es translations for all scheme names and the 46 un-translated values should be added in the next i18n pass. zh+el for the 35 already-translated star/key/epi values to meet the 4-language target. This is consistent with the existing deferred i18n item ('i18n for amenity_family/ref_amenity codes'). Scope: 14 scheme names + 46 value labels in en/es + zh/el for 81 values.

findings.title: ref_sustainability_action has a denormalized category_id column that duplicates the group_id→group.category_id path
findings.evidence: Column introspection shows ref_sustainability_action has BOTH group_id (FK to ref_sustainability_action_group) AND category_id (FK to ref_sustainability_action_category). Since group already has category_id, the action.category_id is redundant. Query confirms: 239/239 have non-null category_id. The source migration (migration_sustainability_v5.sql line 35–38) does NOT add category_id — it was a pre-existing column on the table. Seeds_data.sql inserts populate both group_id and category_id.
findings.recommendation: Document the denormalization as intentional (performance shortcut to avoid a join when filtering by category). Add a comment to the table DDL in schema_unified.sql: '-- category_id is a denormalized cache of group.category_id; kept for query convenience'. Add a constraint or trigger to enforce consistency if not already present. Low priority.

findings.title: Pre-V5 category codes absence confirmed — cleanup guard in place
findings.evidence: Live DB: 0 rows with codes 'energy', 'water', 'waste', 'mobility', 'biodiversity' in ref_sustainability_action_category. seeds_data.sql includes a DO $$ block (lines 1167–1186) that DELETEs pre-V5 categories and then RAISES EXCEPTION if they still exist — providing a run-time idempotency guard. Old label scheme codes 'green_key', 'eu_ecolabel', 'tourisme_handicap', 'destination_excellence', 'qualite_tourisme' absent from ref_classification_scheme (confirmed by query returning 0 rows).
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Confirm guard passes during every fresh-apply gate run. The TST recoding hazard (green_key→LBL_CLEF_VERTE, tourisme_handicap→LBL_TOURISME_HANDICAP) flagged in WORKFLOW.md is correctly documented as deferred — the live DB is clean.
